description:
You are a network engineer who has just logged into a Linux jump-host
  (username: user, home: /home/user) and need to correct several
  symbolic-link problems that are causing the automated connectivity
  tests to select the wrong configuration files and scripts.

  CURRENT FILE-SYSTEM LAYOUT (before you start)
  ├─ /home/user/network_configs/
  │  ├─ siteA_v1/           ← directory, contains the *old* config
  │  ├─ siteA_v2/           ← directory, contains the *new* config
  │  ├─ active  → siteA_v1  ← symbolic link, **points to the wrong version**
  │  └─ symlink_changes.log ← plain text file, may be empty
  ├─ /home/user/bin/
  │  ├─ ping-test   → ../scripts/ping-test.sh  ← valid symlink
  │  └─ ip-test     → ../scripts/ip-test.sh    ← BROKEN symlink
  └─ /home/user/tools/
     └─ ip-test.sh                       ← actual new location of the script

  YOUR GOALS
  1. switch the /home/user/network_configs/active link so that it now
     points to /home/user/network_configs/siteA_v2
  2. create a *new* link named /home/user/network_configs/legacy
     that points to /home/user/network_configs/siteA_v1 (so old tools
     can still reach the legacy config)
  3. repair the broken /home/user/bin/ip-test link so that it resolves
     to /home/user/tools/ip-test.sh
  4. DO NOT touch /home/user/bin/ping-test (it is already correct)
  5. append one single-line entry to
     /home/user/network_configs/symlink_changes.log that documents the
     switch you made in step 1 using **exactly** the following fixed,
     machine-verifiable format (do *not* add extra spaces or characters):

       SYMLINK_CHANGE active siteA_v1 siteA_v2

     • The word order and spacing must match *exactly*.
     • Only one new line should be added.
  6. create a snapshot file at /home/user/symlink_audit_after.txt that
     contains the *verbatim* output of the following command executed **after**
     all fixes are in place (the automated grader will perform a diff):

       find /home/user -maxdepth 3 -type l -ls | sort

     • Redirect only the command’s output, not the prompt or any extra
       explanatory text, into the file.
     • The file must end with a trailing newline.

  VERIFICATION NOTES
  • The grader will check that:
     - /home/user/network_configs/active is a symlink to
       /home/user/network_configs/siteA_v2
     - /home/user/network_configs/legacy is a symlink to
       /home/user/network_configs/siteA_v1
     - /home/user/bin/ip-test is a symlink to /home/user/tools/ip-test.sh
     - /home/user/bin/ping-test remains unchanged
     - /home/user/network_configs/symlink_changes.log contains **exactly**
       one new line: “SYMLINK_CHANGE active siteA_v1 siteA_v2”
     - /home/user/symlink_audit_after.txt exists and its contents are the
       line-sorted output of the above find command executed *after* your
       changes.

  IMPORTANT
  • Do **not** use sudo; you already own everything under /home/user.
  • Make sure you replace the existing “active” link instead of copying
    files around.
  • Do not hard-link; the grader specifically checks for symbolic links.
  • Ensure that both log files are plain ASCII text and writable by the
    current user.

  Once all tasks are complete, simply exit the shell; the automated
  tests will run immediately afterward.
truth:
<!--
  Ground-truth reference for automated grading
  (NOT visible to the agent)
  -->
  <initial_state>
    <dir path="/home/user/network_configs">
      <subdir name="siteA_v1"/>
      <subdir name="siteA_v2"/>
      <symlink name="active" target="siteA_v1"/>
      <file name="symlink_changes.log" contents=""/>
    </dir>
    <dir path="/home/user/bin">
      <symlink name="ping-test" target="../scripts/ping-test.sh"/>
      <symlink name="ip-test" target="../scripts/ip-test.sh"/> <!-- broken -->
    </dir>
    <dir path="/home/user/tools">
      <file name="ip-test.sh" contents="#!/bin/bash\necho 'ip test'\n"/>
    </dir>
  </initial_state>

  <expected_final_state>
    <links>
      <link path="/home/user/network_configs/active" target="/home/user/network_configs/siteA_v2"/>
      <link path="/home/user/network_configs/legacy" target="/home/user/network_configs/siteA_v1"/>
      <link path="/home/user/bin/ip-test" target="/home/user/tools/ip-test.sh"/>
      <link path="/home/user/bin/ping-test" target="../scripts/ping-test.sh"/>
    </links>
    <log path="/home/user/network_configs/symlink_changes.log">
SYMLINK_CHANGE active siteA_v1 siteA_v2
    </log>
    <!-- The exact contents of symlink_audit_after.txt are dynamic but must equal
         the evaluated result of `find /home/user -maxdepth 3 -type l -ls | sort`
         once the links above are in place. -->
  </expected_final_state>
name: task_000000_06845958
